name: Cranberry-Apple Strudle
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 teaspoons cornstarch dissolved in 2 tablespoons cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel, core, and quarter the apples. Cut each quarter in half and then each wedge into three pieces (you will have apple chunks).
2. In a heavy-bottomed 12-inch frying pan, melt 3 tablespoons of butter over medium-high heat. Add the sugar and cook, stirring, until the butter and sugar melt together, turn light brown, and have the consistency of peanut butter.
3. Carefully add the apples to the saucepan (the sugar mixture is very hot). Mix the apples carefully with the caramel using a wooden spoon. The caramel will harden; don't worry, it will melt again.
4. Sprinkle the cinnamon, allspice, and nutmeg evenly over the apples. Cook the apple mixture, stirring often, until the caramel melts and the apples soften and start to look cooked.
5. Stir in the cranberries and cornstarch mixture. Cook for 2 to 3 minutes until the juices in the pan thicken and take on a glossy appearance. Transfer the apple-cranberry mixture to a shallow dish and let cool.
6. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Line a baking sheet with raised rim all around with parchment paper.
7. Melt the remaining 3 tablespoons of butter. Lay a sheet of phyllo on the prepared baking sheet and brush lightly with melted butter. Place the next sheet of phyllo on top and brush it with butter, repeating with the remaining sheets. Do not butter the top sheet; reserve a bit of melted butter to brush on top of the finished strudel.
8. Place the apple-cranberry mixture in a strip along the long side of the phyllo, about 1 inch from the edge. Using the parchment paper to help keep things together, roll up the phyllo to form a long log. Adjust the roll's position on the baking sheet so it is not touching the sides of the sheet. The roll will leak while cooking, so make sure it is on the parchment paper.
9. Brush the top of the roll with the reserved melted butter. Bake in the preheated oven for 25 to 30 minutes or until the roll is nicely browned. Let sit at least 15 minutes before serving.
